title: [Bug]: Oracle MySQL sink accepts empty resolved password environment values
summary: The Oracle MySQL sink resolved password_env values without rejecting empty strings.
observed: An existing but empty environment variable could be treated as a password and fail later as a destination error.
expected: Resolved password environment values must be non-empty.
reproduction: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test for empty password environment values.
impact: Deployment mistakes produce delayed destination failures instead of a clear configuration error.
delivery_semantics: The fix happens before a connection pool is created and does not alter ACK behavior.
security: Secret lookup must fail closed when a configured secret reference resolves to empty data.
acceptance: Oracle MySQL password resolution rejects empty environment values.
Missing environment variables continue to raise a configuration error.
tests: Run the focused Oracle MySQL hardening regression test and the Oracle MySQL unit test suite.
documentation: Document that the resolved password_env value must be non-empty.
closeout: Close only after the release containing the validation fix is published.
